title: ImprovementRecord
description: One record per DEFECTIVE auditor run, emitted by the self-improvement analyzer (OWNER: Codex) from a joined (eval_result, verdict_sidecar) pair. Each record names a single primary failure bucket, the deterministic evidence that fired it, the recommended fix type, and a human-readable diagnosis. Healthy runs (full credit, no flags) produce NO record. The analyzer is a PURE function over schemas/verdict_sidecar.json `signals` — it never reads traces, calls an LLM, or touches the GRPO reward.

reward.description: The run's §1.4 reward, copied through for ranking.

bucket.description: The single primary failure bucket, assigned by the precedence decision tree in the taxonomy design note.

contributing_factors.description: Zero or more secondary buckets that also fired (cross-cutting signals such as artifact_miss / prompt_confusion / weak_fix / fabricated_step_ref). Never contains `bucket`.

fix_type.description: The recommended lever. Exactly one. The current deterministic analyzer (§6) emits only prompt_change | grader_alias_change | dataset_repair | new_training_example. `tool_change` (a §4 discretionary lever when the tool surface is the blocker) and `no_action` (healthy runs produce NO record) are RESERVED for hand-authored records / future analyzer versions — not emitted by the deterministic tree.

confidence.description: high = the bucket was detected directly from a definitive signal; candidate = inferred from a fallback heuristic (e.g. artifact_miss without tool-call data).

severity.description: reward == 0 ⇒ high; partial credit ⇒ medium; full credit but a latent flag tripped ⇒ low.

evidence.description: The specific signal values that fired this bucket (bucket-specific keys; see the taxonomy note). Lets the report show WHY without re-deriving.

suggested_alias.description: Only on bad_failure_type / prompt_confusion driven by an out-of-enum type: { raw_type_key: canonical_failure_type } to add to config.FAILURE_TYPE_ALIASES. Empty/absent otherwise.

diagnosis.description: One deterministic, templated sentence: what went wrong, where, and the signal that proves it.

suggested_action.description: One deterministic, templated sentence: the concrete change to make (matches fix_type).
